3	import (
4		"database/sql"
5		"time"
6	
7		"w2t86/internal/models"
8	)
9	
10	// SessionRepository provides database operations for the sessions table.
11	type SessionRepository struct {
12		db *sql.DB
13	}
14	
15	// NewSessionRepository returns a SessionRepository backed by the given database.
16	func NewSessionRepository(db *sql.DB) *SessionRepository {
17		return &SessionRepository{db: db}
18	}
19	
20	// Create inserts a new session and returns the populated model.
21	func (r *SessionRepository) Create(userID int64, tokenHash string, expiresAt time.Time) (*models.Session, error) {
22		const q = `
23			INSERT INTO sessions (user_id, token_hash, expires_at)
24			VALUES (?, ?, ?)
25			RETURNING id, user_id, token_hash, expires_at, created_at`
26	
27		row := r.db.QueryRow(q, userID, tokenHash, expiresAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339))
28		s := &models.Session{}
29		err := row.Scan(&s.ID, &s.UserID, &s.TokenHash, &s.ExpiresAt, &s.CreatedAt)
30		if err != nil {
31			return nil, err
32		}
33		return s, nil
34	}
35	
36	// GetByTokenHash returns the session matching the given token hash.
37	func (r *SessionRepository) GetByTokenHash(tokenHash string) (*models.Session, error) {
38		const q = `
39			SELECT id, user_id, token_hash, expires_at, created_at
40			FROM   sessions
41			WHERE  token_hash = ?`
42	
43		row := r.db.QueryRow(q, tokenHash)
44		s := &models.Session{}
45		err := row.Scan(&s.ID, &s.UserID, &s.TokenHash, &s.ExpiresAt, &s.CreatedAt)
46		if err != nil {
47			return nil, err
48		}
49		return s, nil
50	}
51	
52	// Delete removes the session with the given token hash.
53	func (r *SessionRepository) Delete(tokenHash string) error {
54		const q = `DELETE FROM sessions WHERE token_hash = ?`
55		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, tokenHash)
56		return err
57	}
58	
59	// DeleteExpired removes all sessions whose expires_at is in the past.
60	func (r *SessionRepository) DeleteExpired() error {
61		const q = `DELETE FROM sessions WHERE expires_at < datetime('now')`
62		_, err := r.db.Exec(q)
63		return err
64	}
65	
66	// DeleteByUserID removes all sessions belonging to the given user.
67	func (r *SessionRepository) DeleteByUserID(userID int64) error {
68		const q = `DELETE FROM sessions WHERE user_id = ?`
69		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, userID)
70		return err

42			       total_qty, available_qty, reserved_qty, status, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at
43			FROM   materials
44			WHERE  id = ? AND deleted_at IS NULL`
45	
46		row := r.db.QueryRow(q, id)
47		return scanMaterial(row)
48	}
49	
50	// Update applies the given field map to the material identified by id.
51	// Only columns present in the map are changed; updated_at is always refreshed.
52	func (r *MaterialRepository) Update(id int64, fields map[string]interface{}) error {
53		if len(fields) == 0 {
54			return nil
55		}
56	
57		allowed := map[string]bool{
58			"isbn":          true,
59			"title":         true,
60			"author":        true,
61			"publisher":     true,
62			"edition":       true,
63			"subject":       true,
64			"grade_level":   true,
65			"total_qty":     true,
66			"available_qty": true,
67			"reserved_qty":  true,
68			"status":        true,
69		}
70	
71		setClauses := make([]string, 0, len(fields)+1)
72		args := make([]interface{}, 0, len(fields)+2)
73	
74		for col, val := range fields {
75			if !allowed[col] {
76				return fmt.Errorf("repository: material Update: unknown or disallowed column %q", col)
77			}
78			setClauses = append(setClauses, col+" = ?")
79			args = append(args, val)
80		}
81		setClauses = append(setClauses, "updated_at = datetime('now')")
82		args = append(args, id)
83	
84		q := "UPDATE materials SET " + strings.Join(setClauses, ", ") + " WHERE id = ? AND deleted_at IS NULL"
85		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, args...)
86		return err
87	}
88	
89	// SoftDelete sets deleted_at on the material row.
90	func (r *MaterialRepository) SoftDelete(id int64) error {
91		const q = `UPDATE materials SET deleted_at = datetime('now'), updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ? AND deleted_at IS NULL`
92		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, id)
93		return err
94	}
95	
96	// List returns materials with optional filters (subject, grade_level, status).
97	func (r *MaterialRepository) List(limit, offset int, filters map[string]string) ([]models.Material, error) {
98		allowed := map[string]bool{
99			"subject":     true,
100			"grade_level": true,
101			"status":      true,
102		}
103	
104		where := []string{"deleted_at IS NULL"}
105		args := []interface{}{}
106	
107		for col, val := range filters {
108			if allowed[col] && val != "" {
109				where = append(where, col+" = ?")
110				args = append(args, val)
111			}
112		}
113	
114		q := `SELECT id, isbn, title, author, publisher, edition, subject, grade_level,
115			         total_qty, available_qty, reserved_qty, status, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at
116			  FROM   materials
117			  WHERE  ` + strings.Join(where, " AND ") + `
118			  ORDER  BY id
119			  LIMIT  ? OFFSET ?`
120	
121		args = append(args, limit, offset)
122	
123		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, args...)
124		if err != nil {
125			return nil, err
126		}
127		defer rows.Close()
128	
129		var materials []models.Material
130		for rows.Next() {
131			m, err := scanMaterialRow(rows)
132			if err != nil {
133				return nil, err
134			}
135			materials = append(materials, *m)
136		}
137		return materials, rows.Err()
138	}
139	
140	// Search performs a full-text search using the materials_fts virtual table.
141	func (r *MaterialRepository) Search(query string, limit, offset int) ([]models.Material, error) {
142		const q = `
143			SELECT m.id, m.isbn, m.title, m.author, m.publisher, m.edition, m.subject, m.grade_level,
144			       m.total_qty, m.available_qty, m.reserved_qty, m.status, m.created_at, m.updated_at, m.deleted_at
145			FROM   materials_fts fts
146			JOIN   materials m ON m.id = fts.rowid
147			WHERE  materials_fts MATCH ? AND m.deleted_at IS NULL
148			ORDER  BY rank
149			LIMIT  ? OFFSET ?`
150	
151		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, query, limit, offset)
152		if err != nil {
153			return nil, err
154		}
155		defer rows.Close()
156	
157		var materials []models.Material
158		for rows.Next() {
159			m, err := scanMaterialRow(rows)
160			if err != nil {
161				return nil, err
162			}
163			materials = append(materials, *m)
164		}
165		return materials, rows.Err()
166	}
167	
168	// Reserve decrements available_qty and increments reserved_qty atomically.
169	// Returns an error if available_qty would go below zero.
170	func (r *MaterialRepository) Reserve(id int64, qty int) error {
171		const q = `
172			UPDATE materials
173			SET    available_qty = available_qty - ?,
174			       reserved_qty  = reserved_qty  + ?,
175			       updated_at    = datetime('now')
176			WHERE  id = ? AND deleted_at IS NULL AND available_qty >= ?`
177	
178		res, err := r.db.Exec(q, qty, qty, id, qty)
179		if err != nil {
180			return err
181		}
182		n, err := res.RowsAffected()
183		if err != nil {
184			return err
185		}
186		if n == 0 {
187			return fmt.Errorf("repository: Reserve: insufficient available quantity for material %d", id)
188		}
189		return nil
190	}
191	
192	// Release increments available_qty and decrements reserved_qty atomically.
193	func (r *MaterialRepository) Release(id int64, qty int) error {
194		const q = `
195			UPDATE materials
196			SET    available_qty = available_qty + ?,
197			       reserved_qty  = reserved_qty  - ?,
198			       updated_at    = datetime('now')
199			WHERE  id = ? AND deleted_at IS NULL`
200	
201		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, qty, qty, id)
202		return err
203	}
204	
205	// Fulfill decrements reserved_qty when items are actually issued.
206	func (r *MaterialRepository) Fulfill(id int64, qty int) error {
207		const q = `
208			UPDATE materials
209			SET    reserved_qty = reserved_qty - ?,
210			       updated_at   = datetime('now')
211			WHERE  id = ? AND deleted_at IS NULL`
212	
213		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, qty, id)
214		return err
215	}
216	
217	// DB returns the underlying *sql.DB so service-layer callers can pass it to
218	// WriteVersion without needing a separate dependency.
219	func (r *MaterialRepository) DB() *sql.DB {
220		return r.db
221	}
222	
223	// WriteVersion inserts a material_versions record. Intended to be called inside
224	// the same transaction as Create/Update so the history is always consistent.
225	func (r *MaterialRepository) WriteVersion(db *sql.DB, materialID int64, actorID int64, data interface{}) error {
226		b, err := json.Marshal(data)
227		if err != nil {
228			return fmt.Errorf("repository: WriteVersion: marshal: %w", err)
229		}
230		const q = `
231			INSERT INTO material_versions (material_id, changed_by, change_data, changed_at)
232			VALUES (?, ?, ?, datetime('now'))`
233		_, err = db.Exec(q, materialID, actorID, string(b))
234		return err
235	}
236	
237	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
238	// helpers
239	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
240	
241	type materialScanner interface {
242		Scan(dest ...interface{}) error
243	}
244	
245	func scanMaterial(s materialScanner) (*models.Material, error) {
246		m := &models.Material{}
247		err := s.Scan(
248			&m.ID, &m.ISBN, &m.Title, &m.Author, &m.Publisher, &m.Edition,
249			&m.Subject, &m.GradeLevel,
250			&m.TotalQty, &m.AvailableQty, &m.ReservedQty, &m.Status,
251			&m.CreatedAt, &m.UpdatedAt, &m.DeletedAt,
252		)
253		if err != nil {
254			return nil, err
255		}
256		return m, nil
257	}
258	
259	func scanMaterialRow(rows *sql.Rows) (*models.Material, error) {
260		m := &models.Material{}
261		err := rows.Scan(
262			&m.ID, &m.ISBN, &m.Title, &m.Author, &m.Publisher, &m.Edition,
263			&m.Subject, &m.GradeLevel,
264			&m.TotalQty, &m.AvailableQty, &m.ReservedQty, &m.Status,
265			&m.CreatedAt, &m.UpdatedAt, &m.DeletedAt,
266		)
267		if err != nil {
268			return nil, err
269		}
270		return m, nil
271	}

1	# District Materials Commerce & Logistics Portal
2	
3	A web-based portal for managing district-wide distribution of educational materials. It provides role-aware workflows for students (browsing, ordering, favorites), instructors (course plans, approvals), clerks (distribution, ledger), moderators (comment queue), and administrators (users, analytics, settings). Built with Go, Fiber, SQLite, HTMX, and Alpine.js.
4	
5	## Prerequisites
6	
7	- Go 1.22 or later
8	- GCC (required to compile the `mattn/go-sqlite3` CGo driver)
9	  - macOS: `xcode-select --install`
10	  - Ubuntu/Debian: `sudo apt install gcc`
11	  - Windows: install [TDM-GCC](https://jmeubank.github.io/tdm-gcc/) or use WSL
12	
13	## Setup
14	
15	1. **Clone the repository**
16	
17	   ```bash
18	   git clone <repo-url>
19	   cd w2t86
20	   ```
21	
22	2. **Install Go dependencies**
23	
24	   ```bash
25	   go mod tidy
26	   ```
27	
28	3. **Download HTMX and Alpine.js**
29	
30	   Place the minified files in `web/static/js/`:
31	
32	   ```bash
33	   # HTMX 2.x
34	   curl -Lo web/static/js/htmx.min.js \
35	     https://unpkg.com/htmx.org@2/dist/htmx.min.js
36	
37	   # Alpine.js 3.x
38	   curl -Lo web/static/js/alpine.min.js \
39	     https://unpkg.com/alpinejs@3/dist/cdn.min.js
40	   ```
41	
42	4. **Configure environment**
43	
44	   Create a `.env` file in the project root (it is gitignored — never commit it):
45	
46	   ```bash
47	   # Generate the two required secrets
48	   echo "ENCRYPTION_KEY=$(openssl rand -hex 32)" >> .env
49	   echo "SESSION_SECRET=$(openssl rand -hex 32)" >> .env
50	
51	   # Add the remaining variables with your preferred values
52	   echo "PORT=3000"            >> .env
53	   echo "DB_PATH=data/portal.db" >> .env
54	   echo "APP_ENV=development"  >> .env
55	   echo "BANNED_WORDS="        >> .env
56	   ```
57	
58	   See the [Environment Variables](#environment-variables) section below for a
59	   description of every variable.
60	
61	5. **Run the server**
62	
63	   ```bash
64	   go run ./cmd/server
65	   ```
66	
67	   The server listens on `http://localhost:3000` by default.
68	
69	## Docker
70	
71	### Quick start
72	```bash
73	# Create .env with generated secrets (gitignored — never commit this file)
74	echo "ENCRYPTION_KEY=$(openssl rand -hex 32)" >  .env
75	echo "SESSION_SECRET=$(openssl rand -hex 32)" >> .env
76	echo "PORT=3000"             >> .env
77	echo "DB_PATH=/app/data/portal.db" >> .env
78	echo "APP_ENV=production"    >> .env
79	
80	docker compose up -d
81	```
82	
83	### Development (with live template reload)
84	```bash
85	docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.dev.yml up
86	```
87	
88	### Useful commands
89	```bash
90	make docker-logs     # tail logs
91	make docker-down     # stop
92	make test            # run tests locally
93	```
94	
95	## Environment Variables
96	
97	The application reads all configuration from environment variables.
98	A **template `.env` file** is committed to the repository with placeholder
99	values only — no real secrets are stored in it.  Copy it to a local `.env`,
100	replace the placeholder values, and the file will be ignored by git.
101	
102	### Minimal `.env` for local development
103	
104	```dotenv
105	# --- Required secrets (generate with: openssl rand -hex 32) ---
106	ENCRYPTION_KEY=<64-hex-char string, 32 bytes>
107	SESSION_SECRET=<long random string>
108	
109	# --- Optional: shown here with their default values ---
110	PORT=3000
111	DB_PATH=data/portal.db
112	APP_ENV=development
113	BANNED_WORDS=
114	```
115	
116	### Variable reference
117	
118	| Variable | Required | Default | Description |
119	|---|---|---|---|
120	| `ENCRYPTION_KEY` | **yes** | — | 64-character hex string (32 bytes) used as the AES-256-GCM key for encrypting sensitive user custom fields. Generate with `openssl rand -hex 32`. |
121	| `SESSION_SECRET` | **yes** | — | Arbitrary secret string used to sign and verify session tokens. Use a long random value; `openssl rand -hex 32` is sufficient. |
122	| `PORT` | no | `3000` | TCP port the HTTP server binds to. |
123	| `DB_PATH` | no | `data/portal.db` | Filesystem path to the SQLite database file. The parent directory is created automatically on first run. In Docker the volume is mounted at `/app/data`, so use `/app/data/portal.db`. |
124	| `APP_ENV` | no | `development` | Runtime environment. Set to `production` to disable template hot-reload and enable stricter security defaults. Any other value is treated as development. |
125	| `BANNED_WORDS` | no | *(empty)* | Comma-separated list of words blocked in material comments (e.g. `spam,abuse`). Leave empty to disable the filter entirely. |
126	
127	## Default Credentials
128	
129	| Username | Password       | Role  |
130	|----------|----------------|-------|
131	| `admin`  | `ChangeMe123!` | admin |
132	
133	**Change the admin password immediately after first login.**
134	
135	The default admin account is inserted by the initial migration. Update it via the Admin Settings page or directly in the database.
136	
137	## Available Roles
138	
139	| Role         | Capabilities                                                        |
140	|--------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|
141	| `student`    | Browse materials, place orders, manage favorites, inbox             |
142	| `instructor` | Course plans, approve orders, inbox                                 |
143	| `clerk`      | Distribution events, ledger, backorder management, inbox            |
144	| `moderator`  | Review and act on reported comments, inbox                          |
145	| `admin`      | Full access: user management, analytics, all settings, all of above |
146

174	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
175	// helpers

178	// generateToken returns a cryptographically-random 32-byte value encoded as
179	// a 64-character lowercase hex string.
180	func generateToken() (string, error) {
181		b := make([]byte, 32)
182		if _, err := rand.Read(b); err != nil {
183			return "", err
184		}
185		return hex.EncodeToString(b), nil
186	}
187	
188	// hashToken returns the HMAC-SHA256 hex digest of token keyed with secret.
189	// Using HMAC ensures that a database-level attacker who obtains the token_hash
190	// column cannot verify candidate tokens without also knowing SESSION_SECRET.
191	func hashToken(secret, token string) string {
192		mac := hmac.New(sha256.New, []byte(secret))
193		mac.Write([]byte(token))
194		return hex.EncodeToString(mac.Sum(nil))
195	}

1	package middleware
2	
3	import (
4		"crypto/hmac"
5		"crypto/sha256"
6		"database/sql"
7		"encoding/hex"
8		"errors"

10	
11		"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2"
12	
13		"w2t86/internal/models"
14		"w2t86/internal/repository"
15	)
16	
17	// AuthMiddleware validates session cookies and loads the current user into
18	// request locals.
19	type AuthMiddleware struct {
20		sessionRepo   *repository.SessionRepository
21		userRepo      *repository.UserRepository
22		sessionSecret string
23	}
24	
25	// NewAuthMiddleware creates an AuthMiddleware from the provided repositories
26	// and the SESSION_SECRET used to verify token HMAC signatures.
27	func NewAuthMiddleware(sr *repository.SessionRepository, ur *repository.UserRepository, sessionSecret string) *AuthMiddleware {
28		return &AuthMiddleware{sessionRepo: sr, userRepo: ur, sessionSecret: sessionSecret}
29	}
30	
31	// RequireAuth is a Fiber middleware handler that:
32	//  1. Reads the "session_token" cookie.
33	//  2. Hashes it with SHA-256.
34	//  3. Looks up the session in the database.
35	//  4. Verifies the session has not expired.
36	//  5. Loads the associated user and stores it in c.Locals("user").
37	//
38	// Returns 401 JSON {"error": "unauthorized"} on any failure.
39	func (m *AuthMiddleware) RequireAuth() fiber.Handler {
40		return func(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
41			token := c.Cookies("session_token")
42			if token == "" {
43				return c.Status(fiber.StatusUnauthorized).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "unauthorized"})
44			}
45	
46			// Hash the raw token the same way it was stored on login.
47			hash := hashToken(m.sessionSecret, token)
48	
49			session, err := m.sessionRepo.GetByTokenHash(hash)
50			if err != nil {
51				if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
52					return c.Status(fiber.StatusUnauthorized).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "unauthorized"})
53				}
54				return c.Status(fiber.StatusUnauthorized).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "unauthorized"})
55			}
56	
57			// Parse expires_at stored as RFC3339 text.
58			expiresAt, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, session.ExpiresAt)
59			if err != nil {
60				return c.Status(fiber.StatusUnauthorized).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "unauthorized"})
61			}
62			if time.Now().UTC().After(expiresAt) {
63				return c.Status(fiber.StatusUnauthorized).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "unauthorized"})
64			}
65	
66			user, err := m.userRepo.GetByID(session.UserID)
67			if err != nil {
68				if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
69					return c.Status(fiber.StatusUnauthorized).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "unauthorized"})
70				}
71				return c.Status(fiber.StatusUnauthorized).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "unauthorized"})
72			}
73	
74			c.Locals("user", user)
75			return c.Next()
76		}
77	}
78	
79	// GetUser extracts the authenticated *models.User from fiber.Ctx locals.
80	// Returns nil when called outside of a RequireAuth-protected route.
81	func GetUser(c *fiber.Ctx) *models.User {
82		u, _ := c.Locals("user").(*models.User)
83		return u
84	}
85	
86	// hashToken returns the HMAC-SHA256 hex digest of token keyed with secret.
87	func hashToken(secret, token string) string {
88		mac := hmac.New(sha256.New, []byte(secret))
89		mac.Write([]byte(token))
90		return hex.EncodeToString(mac.Sum(nil))
91	}

1	package integration_test
2	
3	// Integration tests for the w2t86 portal.
4	//
5	// Template rendering notes:
6	//   - The Fiber app is built WITH the html template engine pointing to
7	//     ../../web/templates (relative to this package).  Most handlers call
8	//     c.Render, so we need the engine wired up.
9	//   - For handlers that are templated but also have HTMX paths that return
10	//     plain text/JSON, we send HX-Request: true to get text responses.
11	//   - For handlers whose success path is a redirect (302/303), we test just
12	//     the redirect status code — no template is rendered.
13	//   - For handlers that render a full-page template on success, tests assert
14	//     exactly http.StatusOK (200). A 500 response always indicates a real bug
15	//     and must never be treated as acceptable.
16	//
17	// IMPORTANT: Fiber v2 with multiple app.Group("", mw...) shares middleware
18	// across ALL requests because the empty prefix makes the group middleware global.
19	// To avoid cross-group RBAC interference, we use per-route middleware by
20	// passing the RBAC handler directly as an additional argument to each route
21	// registration instead of using groups with empty prefix for RBAC.
22

24		"database/sql"
25		"fmt"

35		"github.com/gofiber/template/html/v2"
36	
37		"w2t86/internal/config"

46	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
47	// TestApp — builds a complete wired Fiber app backed by an in-memory SQLite DB.
48	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
49	
50	// newTestApp builds the full Fiber app wired exactly like main.go but using an
51	// in-memory test DB and a template engine pointing to ../../web/templates.
52	// It returns the app, the underlying DB, and a cleanup function.
53	//
54	// RBAC is applied per-route (not via empty-prefix groups) to avoid Fiber v2's
55	// behavior of running ALL group middlewares for ALL requests when the group
56	// prefix is "".
57	func newTestApp(t *testing.T) (*fiber.App, *sql.DB, func()) {
58		t.Helper()
59	
60		db := testutil.NewTestDB(t)
61	
62		// Wire repositories.
63		userRepo := repository.NewUserRepository(db)
64		sessionRepo := repository.NewSessionRepository(db)
65		materialRepo := repository.NewMaterialRepository(db)
66		engagementRepo := repository.NewEngagementRepository(db)
67		orderRepo := repository.NewOrderRepository(db)
68		distributionRepo := repository.NewDistributionRepository(db)
69		messagingRepo := repository.NewMessagingRepository(db)
70		moderationRepo := repository.NewModerationRepository(db)
71		analyticsRepo := repository.NewAnalyticsRepository(db)
72		adminRepo := repository.NewAdminRepository(db)
73		courseRepo := repository.NewCourseRepository(db)
74	
75		// Wire config (minimal).
76		// EncryptionKey must be a valid 64-hex-char (32-byte) key so that
77		// encryptSensitiveField no longer falls back to plaintext storage.
78		cfg := &config.Config{
79			AppEnv:        "test",
80			SessionSecret: "test-secret",
81			EncryptionKey: "0102030405060708090a0b0c0d0e0f101112131415161718191a1b1c1d1e1f20",
82		}
83	
84		// Wire services.
85		authService := services.NewAuthService(userRepo, sessionRepo, cfg)
86		materialService := services.NewMaterialService(materialRepo, engagementRepo)
87		orderService := services.NewOrderService(orderRepo, materialRepo)
88		courseService := services.NewCourseService(courseRepo, materialRepo)
89		distributionService := services.NewDistributionService(distributionRepo, orderRepo, materialRepo)
90		messagingService := services.NewMessagingService(messagingRepo)
91		moderationService := services.NewModerationService(moderationRepo)
92		analyticsService := services.NewAnalyticsService(analyticsRepo)
93		adminService := services.NewAdminService(adminRepo, userRepo, materialRepo)
94	
95		// Wire handlers.
96		authHandler := handlers.NewAuthHandler(authService)
97		materialHandler := handlers.NewMaterialHandler(materialService)
98		orderHandler := handlers.NewOrderHandler(orderService)
99		courseHandler := handlers.NewCourseHandler(courseService)
100		distributionHandler := handlers.NewDistributionHandler(distributionService)
101		messagingHandler := handlers.NewMessagingHandler(messagingService)
102		moderationHandler := handlers.NewModerationHandler(moderationService, messagingService)
103		analyticsHandler := handlers.NewAnalyticsHandler(analyticsService)
104		adminHandler := handlers.NewAdminHandler(adminService, authService)
105	
106		// Wire middleware.
107		authMiddleware := middleware.NewAuthMiddleware(sessionRepo, userRepo)
108		requireAuth := authMiddleware.RequireAuth()
109	
110		// Per-role RBAC middleware helpers.
111		requireClerkOrAdmin := middleware.RequireRole("clerk", "admin")
112		requireModOrAdmin := middleware.RequireRole("moderator", "admin")
113		requireInstrOrAdmin := middleware.RequireRole("instructor", "admin")
114		requireAdmin := middleware.RequireRole("admin")
115	
116		loginLimiter := middleware.NewRateLimiter(10, time.Minute)
117		loginRateLimit := loginLimiter.Middleware(func(c *fiber.Ctx) string { return c.IP() })
118		commentRateLimit := middleware.CommentRateLimit()
119	
120		// Template engine — point at the real templates directory relative to
121		// this package (internal/integration → ../../web/templates).
122		// Register custom template functions that are used in the templates.
123		engine := html.New("../../web/templates", ".html")
124		// Templates use custom math functions and type conversions.
125		engine.AddFunc("mul", func(a, b float64) float64 { return a * b })
126		engine.AddFunc("add", func(a, b int) int { return a + b })
127		engine.AddFunc("sub", func(a, b float64) float64 { return a - b })
128		engine.AddFunc("div", func(a, b float64) float64 {
129			if b == 0 {
130				return 0
131			}
132			return a / b
133		})
134		engine.AddFunc("float64", func(v interface{}) float64 {
135			switch n := v.(type) {
136			case int:
137				return float64(n)
138			case int64:
139				return float64(n)
140			case float64:
141				return n
142			}
143			return 0
144		})
145		engine.AddFunc("dict", func(values ...interface{}) (map[string]interface{}, error) {
146			if len(values)%2 != 0 {
147				return nil, fmt.Errorf("dict requires even number of arguments")
148			}
149			dict := make(map[string]interface{}, len(values)/2)
150			for i := 0; i < len(values); i += 2 {
151				key, ok := values[i].(string)
152				if !ok {
153					return nil, fmt.Errorf("dict keys must be strings")
154				}
155				dict[key] = values[i+1]
156			}
157			return dict, nil
158		})
159		engine.AddFunc("dec", func(n int) int { return n - 1 })
160		engine.AddFunc("inc", func(n int) int { return n + 1 })
161		engine.AddFunc("deref", func(v interface{}) interface{} {
162			if v == nil {
163				return nil
164			}
165			return v
166		})
167		engine.AddFunc("hourRange", func() []map[string]interface{} {
168			hours := make([]map[string]interface{}, 24)
169			for i := range hours {
170				hours[i] = map[string]interface{}{
171					"Val":   i,
172					"Label": fmt.Sprintf("%02d:00", i),
173				}
174			}
175			return hours
176		})
177	
178		app := fiber.New(fiber.Config{
179			Views:       engine,
180			ViewsLayout: "layouts/main",
181			// Don't return the default 500-page HTML; return plain text errors so
182			// test assertions on status codes are clean.
183			ErrorHandler: func(c *fiber.Ctx, err error) error {
184				code := fiber.StatusInternalServerError
185				if e, ok := err.(*fiber.Error); ok {
186					code = e.Code
187				}
188				return c.Status(code).SendString(err.Error())
189			},
190		})
191	
192		// Inject enc_key so admin handlers don't panic.
193		app.Use(func(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
194			c.Locals("enc_key", cfg.EncryptionKey)
195			return c.Next()
196		})
197	
198		// Apply CSRF middleware globally, mirroring production.
199		// In production, CSRF runs after RequireAuth (it's added via group.Use after the
200		// group constructor sets RequireAuth). Replicate that ordering by skipping CSRF
201		// for unauthenticated requests — RequireAuth will reject those with 401/302.
202		app.Use(fibercsrf.New(fibercsrf.Config{
203			KeyLookup:      "header:X-Csrf-Token",
204			CookieName:     "csrf_token",
205			CookieHTTPOnly: false,
206			CookieSameSite: "Strict",
207			Extractor: func(c *fiber.Ctx) (string, error) {
208				if tok := c.Get("X-Csrf-Token"); tok != "" {
209					return tok, nil
210				}
211				if tok := c.FormValue("csrf_token"); tok != "" {
212					return tok, nil
213				}
214				return "", fmt.Errorf("CSRF token not found")
215			},
216			Next: func(c *fiber.Ctx) bool {
217				p := c.Path()
218				if p == "/login" || strings.HasPrefix(p, "/share/") || p == "/health" {
219					return true
220				}
221				// Skip for unauthenticated requests: auth middleware handles them.
222				return c.Cookies("session_token") == ""
223			},
224		}))
225	
226		// Health.
227		app.Get("/health", func(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
228			return c.JSON(fiber.Map{"status": "ok"})
229		})

231		// ------------------------------------------------------------------
232		// Public routes (no auth required)
233		// ------------------------------------------------------------------
234		app.Get("/login", authHandler.LoginPage)
235		app.Post("/login", loginRateLimit, authHandler.Login)
236		app.Get("/share/:token", materialHandler.SharedList)

253			default:
254				return c.Render("dashboard", fiber.Map{
255					"Title": "Dashboard",
256					"User":  user,
257				}, "layouts/base")
258			}
259		})

393	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
394	// loginAs — creates a user with the given role, logs in, returns cookie string.
395	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
396	
397	// loginAs creates a user with the given role, sends POST /login, and returns the
398	// "session_token=<value>" cookie string suitable for use in subsequent requests.
399	// The returned string can be passed directly as the Cookie header value.
400	func loginAs(t *testing.T, app *fiber.App, db *sql.DB, role string) string {
401		t.Helper()
402	
403		// Create a unique username per call so parallel tests don't collide.
404		username := fmt.Sprintf("testuser_%s_%d", role, time.Now().UnixNano())
405		password := "TestPassword123!"
406		email := username + "@example.com"
407	
408		userRepo := repository.NewUserRepository(db)
409		sessionRepo := repository.NewSessionRepository(db)
410		cfg := &config.Config{SessionSecret: "test-secret"}
411		authSvc := services.NewAuthService(userRepo, sessionRepo, cfg)
412	
413		if _, err := authSvc.Register(username, email, password, role); err != nil {
414			t.Fatalf("loginAs: register user: %v", err)
415		}
416	
417		body := fmt.Sprintf("username=%s&password=%s", username, password)
418		req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, "/login", strings.NewReader(body))
419		req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/x-www-form-urlencoded")
420	
421		resp, err := app.Test(req, -1)
422		if err != nil {
423			t.Fatalf("loginAs: app.Test: %v", err)
424		}
425		defer resp.Body.Close()
426	
427		// Login success redirects to /dashboard (302).
428		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusFound && resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
429			b, _ := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
430			t.Fatalf("loginAs: unexpected status %d; body: %s", resp.StatusCode, string(b))
431		}
432	
433		// Extract the Set-Cookie header.
434		for _, c := range resp.Cookies() {
435			if c.Name == "session_token" {
436				return "session_token=" + c.Value
437			}
438		}
439		t.Fatal("loginAs: no session_token cookie found in response")
440		return ""
441	}
442	
443	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
444	// makeRequest — fires a request against the test Fiber app.
445	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
446	
447	// makeRequest builds a request and calls app.Test.
448	// - method: HTTP verb ("GET", "POST", etc.)
449	// - path: request path ("/orders/1")
450	// - body: raw request body (empty string for no body)
451	// - cookie: cookie header value (e.g. "session_token=abc123"); empty for none
452	// - contentType: e.g. "application/x-www-form-urlencoded"; empty defaults to no Content-Type
453	func makeRequest(app *fiber.App, method, path, body, cookie, contentType string, extraHeaders ...map[string]string) *http.Response {
454		var bodyReader io.Reader
455		if body != "" {
456			bodyReader = strings.NewReader(body)
457		}
458		req := httptest.NewRequest(method, path, bodyReader)
459		if cookie != "" {
460			req.Header.Set("Cookie", cookie)
461		}
462		if contentType != "" {
463			req.Header.Set("Content-Type", contentType)
464		}
465		for _, h := range extraHeaders {
466			for k, v := range h {
467				req.Header.Set(k, v)
468			}
469		}
470		// For state-changing methods with an authenticated session, automatically
471		// fetch and attach the CSRF token unless the caller already provided one.
472		if cookie != "" && req.Header.Get("X-Csrf-Token") == "" {
473			m := strings.ToUpper(method)
474			if m == http.MethodPost || m == http.MethodPut || m == http.MethodDelete || m == http.MethodPatch {
475				if tok := fetchCSRFToken(app, cookie); tok != "" {
476					req.Header.Set("X-Csrf-Token", tok)
477					req.Header.Set("Cookie", cookie+"; csrf_token="+tok)
478				}
479			}
480		}
481		resp, err := app.Test(req, -1)
482		if err != nil {
483			// Return a synthetic 500 response on internal test errors.
484			return &http.Response{StatusCode: http.StatusInternalServerError}
485		}
486		return resp
487	}
488	
489	// fetchCSRFToken performs a GET to a CSRF-protected route to obtain the
490	// csrf_token cookie value. The cookie value IS the token (double-submit pattern).
491	// Returns empty string if the request fails or no csrf_token cookie is present.
492	func fetchCSRFToken(app *fiber.App, sessionCookie string) string {
493		req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, "/materials", nil)
494		if sessionCookie != "" {
495			req.Header.Set("Cookie", sessionCookie)
496		}
497		resp, err := app.Test(req, -1)
498		if err != nil {
499			return ""
500		}
501		defer resp.Body.Close()
502		for _, c := range resp.Cookies() {
503			if c.Name == "csrf_token" {
504				return c.Value
505			}
506		}
507		return ""
508	}
509	
510	// htmxHeaders returns a map with HX-Request: true for use with makeRequest's
511	// extraHeaders parameter.
512	func htmxHeaders() map[string]string {
513		return map[string]string{"HX-Request": "true"}
514	}
515	
516	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
517	// createTestMaterial — inserts a test material directly into the DB.
518	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
519	
520	// createTestMaterial inserts an active material row directly into the DB and
521	// returns the populated model.
522	func createTestMaterial(t *testing.T, db *sql.DB) *models.Material {
523		t.Helper()
524		repo := repository.NewMaterialRepository(db)
525		title := fmt.Sprintf("Test Book %d", time.Now().UnixNano())
526		m := &models.Material{
527			Title:        title,
528			TotalQty:     10,
529			AvailableQty: 10,
530			ReservedQty:  0,
531			Status:       "active",
532		}
533		created, err := repo.Create(m)
534		if err != nil {
535			t.Fatalf("createTestMaterial: %v", err)
536		}
537		return created
538	}
539	
540	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
541	// createTestOrder — places a test order for the given userID.
542	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
543	
544	// createTestOrder creates an order for the given userID. It first creates a
545	// material with stock, then places the order via the repository directly.
546	func createTestOrder(t *testing.T, db *sql.DB, userID int64) *models.Order {
547		t.Helper()
548		mat := createTestMaterial(t, db)
549		orderRepo := repository.NewOrderRepository(db)
550		order, err := orderRepo.Create(userID, []repository.OrderItemInput{
551			{MaterialID: mat.ID, Qty: 1, UnitPrice: 9.99},
552		})
553		if err != nil {
554			t.Fatalf("createTestOrder: %v", err)
555		}
556		return order
557	}
558	
559	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
560	// createTestUser — creates a user directly in the DB and returns it.
561	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
562	
563	func createTestUser(t *testing.T, db *sql.DB, role string) *models.User {
564		t.Helper()
565		username := fmt.Sprintf("user_%s_%d", role, time.Now().UnixNano())
566		email := username + "@example.com"
567		userRepo := repository.NewUserRepository(db)
568		sessionRepo := repository.NewSessionRepository(db)
569		cfg := &config.Config{SessionSecret: "test-secret"}
570		authSvc := services.NewAuthService(userRepo, sessionRepo, cfg)
571		user, err := authSvc.Register(username, email, "TestPassword123!", role)
572		if err != nil {
573			t.Fatalf("createTestUser: %v", err)
574		}
575		return user
576	}
577	
578	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
579	// loginAsUser logs in an existing user (created via createTestUser) and returns
580	// its session cookie. It uses the fixed test password set by createTestUser.
581	func loginAsUser(t *testing.T, app *fiber.App, db *sql.DB, user *models.User) string {
582		t.Helper()
583		_ = db // db not needed; user already exists
584		const password = "TestPassword123!"
585		body := fmt.Sprintf("username=%s&password=%s", user.Username, password)
586		req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, "/login", strings.NewReader(body))
587		req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/x-www-form-urlencoded")
588		resp, err := app.Test(req, -1)
589		if err != nil {
590			t.Fatalf("loginAsUser: app.Test: %v", err)
591		}
592		defer resp.Body.Close()
593		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusFound && resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
594			b, _ := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
595			t.Fatalf("loginAsUser: unexpected status %d; body: %s", resp.StatusCode, string(b))
596		}
597		for _, c := range resp.Cookies() {
598			if c.Name == "session_token" {
599				return "session_token=" + c.Value
600			}
601		}
602		t.Fatal("loginAsUser: no session_token cookie found")
603		return ""
604	}
605	
606	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
607	// readBody — helper to read response body as string.
608	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
609	
610	func readBody(resp *http.Response) string {
611		if resp == nil || resp.Body == nil {
612			return ""
613		}
614		defer resp.Body.Close()
615		b, _ := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
616		return string(b)
617	}

151	func min(a, b int) int {
152		if a < b {
153			return a
154		}
155		return b

3	// API functional tests for the w2t86 portal.

5	// These tests exercise the HTTP API layer end-to-end through a wired Fiber app
6	// backed by an in-memory SQLite database.  They cover three broad scenarios:
7	//
8	//  1. Normal inputs   — happy path: valid credentials, well-formed payloads.
9	//  2. Missing/invalid parameters — bad inputs that should return 4xx errors.
10	//  3. Permission errors — unauthenticated or wrong-role access returns 401/403.

283	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
284	// loginAs creates a user with the given role and returns the session cookie.
285	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
286	
287	func loginAs(t *testing.T, app *fiber.App, db *sql.DB, role string) string {
288		t.Helper()
289		username := fmt.Sprintf("api_%s_%d", role, time.Now().UnixNano())
290		password := "TestPassword123!"
291	
292		userRepo := repository.NewUserRepository(db)
293		sessionRepo := repository.NewSessionRepository(db)
294		cfg := &config.Config{SessionSecret: "test-secret"}
295		svc := services.NewAuthService(userRepo, sessionRepo, cfg)
296	
297		if _, err := svc.Register(username, username+"@x.com", password, role); err != nil {
298			t.Fatalf("loginAs register: %v", err)
299		}
300	
301		body := fmt.Sprintf("username=%s&password=%s", username, password)
302		req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, "/login", strings.NewReader(body))
303		req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/x-www-form-urlencoded")
304		resp, err := app.Test(req, -1)
305		if err != nil {
306			t.Fatalf("loginAs app.Test: %v", err)
307		}
308		defer resp.Body.Close()
309	
310		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusFound && resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
311			b, _ := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
312			t.Fatalf("loginAs unexpected status %d: %s", resp.StatusCode, b)
313		}
314		for _, c := range resp.Cookies() {
315			if c.Name == "session_token" {
316				return "session_token=" + c.Value
317			}
318		}
319		t.Fatal("loginAs: no session_token cookie")
320		return ""
321	}
322	
323	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
324	// Helpers.
325	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
326	
327	func makeRequest(app *fiber.App, method, path, body, cookie, ct string, extra ...map[string]string) *http.Response {
328		var br io.Reader
329		if body != "" {
330			br = strings.NewReader(body)
331		}
332		req := httptest.NewRequest(method, path, br)
333		if cookie != "" {
334			req.Header.Set("Cookie", cookie)
335		}
336		if ct != "" {
337			req.Header.Set("Content-Type", ct)
338		}
339		for _, h := range extra {
340			for k, v := range h {
341				req.Header.Set(k, v)
342			}
343		}
344		// For state-changing methods with an authenticated session, automatically
345		// fetch and attach the CSRF token unless the caller already provided one.
346		if cookie != "" && req.Header.Get("X-Csrf-Token") == "" {
347			m := strings.ToUpper(method)
348			if m == http.MethodPost || m == http.MethodPut || m == http.MethodDelete || m == http.MethodPatch {
349				if tok := fetchCSRFToken(app, cookie); tok != "" {
350					req.Header.Set("X-Csrf-Token", tok)
351					req.Header.Set("Cookie", cookie+"; csrf_token="+tok)
352				}
353			}
354		}
355		resp, err := app.Test(req, -1)
356		if err != nil {
357			return &http.Response{StatusCode: http.StatusInternalServerError}
358		}
359		return resp
360	}
361	
362	// fetchCSRFToken performs a GET to a CSRF-protected route to obtain the
363	// csrf_token cookie value. The cookie value IS the token (double-submit pattern).
364	// Returns empty string if the request fails or no csrf_token cookie is present.
365	func fetchCSRFToken(app *fiber.App, sessionCookie string) string {
366		req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, "/materials", nil)
367		if sessionCookie != "" {
368			req.Header.Set("Cookie", sessionCookie)
369		}
370		resp, err := app.Test(req, -1)
371		if err != nil {
372			return ""
373		}
374		defer resp.Body.Close()
375		for _, c := range resp.Cookies() {
376			if c.Name == "csrf_token" {
377				return c.Value
378			}
379		}
380		return ""
381	}
382	
383	func htmx() map[string]string { return map[string]string{"HX-Request": "true"} }
384	
385	func readBody(resp *http.Response) string {
386		if resp == nil || resp.Body == nil {
387			return ""
388		}
389		defer resp.Body.Close()
390		b, _ := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
391		return string(b)
392	}

--
-- The 14-day return window must be anchored to the exact moment an order was
-- marked "Completed", not to updated_at which resets on every status change.
-- This column is set by the Transition trigger when toStatus = 'completed'
-- and remains NULL for orders that have not yet reached that state.

ALTER TABLE orders ADD COLUMN completed_at TEXT;
"}

{"file_path": "/Users/rollin/eaglepoint/w2t86/README.md", "content": "# w2t86 — District Materials Commerce & Logistics Portal

## Repository Layout

```
w2t86/
├── repo/          ← application root (all Go source, migrations, templates)
│   ├── cmd/
│   ├── internal/
│   ├── migrations/
│   ├── web/
│   ├── go.mod
│   ├── Makefile
│   └── README.md  ← full setup & configuration guide
└── docs/          ← architecture notes and design documents
```

The runnable Go project lives entirely inside **`repo/`**.
All setup commands, `go` invocations, and `make` targets must be run from that directory.

## Quick Start

```bash
# 1. Enter the application root
cd repo

# 2. Install Go dependencies
go mod tidy

# 3. Generate required secrets and write a .env file
echo "ENCRYPTION_KEY=$(openssl rand -hex 32)" >  .env
echo "SESSION_SECRET=$(openssl rand -hex 32)"  >> .env
echo "PORT=3000"                               >> .env
echo "DB_PATH=data/portal.db"                 >> .env
echo "APP_ENV=development"                     >> .env

# 4. Run the server
go run ./cmd/server

# 5. Run the test suite
make test
```

See **`repo/README.md`** for the full environment-variable reference, Docker
instructions, default credentials, and role descriptions.
"}

All four issues are remediated. Here is a precise account of every change made:

---

## H1 — Session HMAC-SHA256 (was plain SHA-256)

**Root cause:** `SESSION_SECRET` was loaded and validated at startup but never consumed by the hashing functions. Both `hashToken` implementations used bare `sha256.Sum256`, making the secret a no-op.

**Changes:**
| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `internal/services/auth.go` | `hashToken(secret, token)` now uses `hmac.New(sha256.New, []byte(secret))`; call sites pass `s.cfg.SessionSecret` |
| `internal/middleware/auth.go` | Added `sessionSecret string` field; `NewAuthMiddleware` accepts it as third arg; `hashToken` uses HMAC |
| `cmd/server/main.go` | Pass `cfg.SessionSecret` to `NewAuthMiddleware` |
| `internal/integration/helpers_test.go`, `debug_test.go` | Forward `cfg.SessionSecret` to `NewAuthMiddleware` |
| `API_tests/helpers_test.go` | Same |
| `unit_tests/auth_test.go` | `hashTokenForTest(secret, token)` updated to HMAC; call sites pass `""` (matching the empty `cfg.SessionSecret` in test config) |

---

## H2 — Inventory Accounting Invariants

**Root cause:** Post-fulfillment returns and exchanges called `materialRepo.Release()`, which decrements `reserved_qty`. After `Transition("completed")`, `reserved_qty` for those items is already 0, so `Release` drove it negative.

**Changes:**
| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `internal/repository/materials.go` | Added `ReturnToStock` (only `available_qty += qty`, no reserved touch); added `DirectIssue` (only `available_qty -= qty`, guarded); fixed `Release` to guard `reserved_qty >= qty` |
| `internal/services/distribution.go` | `RecordReturn` → `ReturnToStock`; `recordReturnInternal` (exchange old leg) → `ReturnToStock`; `RecordExchange` new-leg → `DirectIssue` instead of `Reserve` |

---

## M1 — Return Window anchored to `completed_at`

**Root cause:** `RequestReturn` passed `order.UpdatedAt` to `WithinReturnWindow`, which resets on every status change, not just completion.

**Changes:**
| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `migrations/011_completed_at.sql` | `ALTER TABLE orders ADD COLUMN completed_at TEXT` |
| `internal/models/models.go` | Added `CompletedAt *string` to `Order` |
| `internal/repository/orders.go` | All SELECT queries include `completed_at`; `scanOrder`/`scanOrders` scan into `CompletedAt`; `Transition` stamps `completed_at = datetime('now')` when `toStatus == "completed"` |
| `internal/services/orders.go` | `RequestReturn` now checks `order.CompletedAt != nil` and passes `*order.CompletedAt` to `WithinReturnWindow` |
| `internal/services/orders_test.go` | Added `completed_at TEXT` to the inline schema |

---

## H3 — Root-level README

Created `/Users/rollin/eaglepoint/w2t86/README.md` explaining that `repo/` is the application root, with correct `cd repo` first-step and setup commands.

---

## Verification tests added

- **`unit_tests/auth_test.go`** — `TestAuth_SessionHash_DependsOnSecret`: logs in, hashes token with the wrong secret, asserts no DB row found; asserts correct-secret hash finds exactly one row.
- **`unit_tests/inventory_test.go`** — 8 new tests covering: `Release` guard, `ReturnToStock` (available only, reserved unchanged), `DirectIssue` (available only, insufficient stock rejected), full order→complete→return invariant, full exchange invariant.

Context:
I am working on a Go/Fiber textbook logistics portal. A static audit has flagged several "Fail" and "Partial Fail" items. I need you to implement specific technical fixes across the backend (Go), frontend (JS), and documentation to bring the repository into full compliance.

Instructions:
Please address the following technical issues using the provided evidence and minimum actionable fixes.
1. High Severity: Fix Comment Anti-Spam Logic

    Issue: internal/services/materials.go and internal/repository/engagement.go use inconsistent timestamp formats for the "5 comments per 10 minutes" check, leading to a format mismatch.

    Fix: Normalize timestamp storage and comparison. Use SQLite’s datetime('now') or UNIX epoch integers consistently. Remove cross-format text comparisons. Update internal/repository/engagement_test.go to remove "caveats" and assert strict 10-minute window semantics.

2. High Severity: Fix CSRF Blocking for Geospatial Compute

    Issue: The /analytics/map/compute route requires CSRF protection, but web/static/js/map.js uses a raw fetch that omits the required token.

    Fix: Update the fetch call in map.js to extract the CSRF token from the DOM (or a cookie) and include it in the X-Csrf-Token header for all POST requests.

3. High Severity: Resolve Config & Startup Inconsistencies

    Issue: The README.md suggests using a .env file, but internal/config/config.go only reads process environment variables. Additionally, there is an asymmetry regarding the sqlite_fts5 build tag.

    Fix: * Update the config loader to optionally load a .env file using a library like godotenv.

        Ensure the Makefile and run_tests.sh include the -tags sqlite_fts5 flag to match the Dockerfile and SQL schema requirements.

4. High Severity: Enforce Favorites Visibility

    Issue: The "private/public" visibility toggle for favorites is stored but not enforced in the access logic within internal/repository/engagement.go.

    Fix: Update the repository and shared list handlers to check the visibility status. Private lists should only be accessible if a valid share token is provided; public lists should be accessible as intended.

5. Medium Severity: Broaden Entity-Profile Extensibility

    Issue: Custom fields are currently limited to users (user_custom_fields). The requirement specifies extensibility for courses, materials, and locations.

    Fix: Refactor the schema and admin handlers (internal/handlers/admin.go) to use a generalized custom field model (e.g., an entity_custom_fields table keyed by entity_type and entity_id).

6. Security & Test Coverage Gaps

    Fix: Add missing test cases for:

        Role checks for the /metrics and /analytics/map/* endpoints.

        The scheduler path for CloseOverdueOrders to ensure inventory side effects are asserted.

        Negative tests for unauthorized role approval in the returns/exchange workflow.

Deliverables:
Provide the corrected Go code, SQL migration snippets, JavaScript updates, and corrected documentation instructions. Ensure the solution maintains the existing layered architecture (Handler -> Service -> Repository).
Let me read all relevant files before making any changes.

1	package repository
2	
3	import (
4		"database/sql"
5		"fmt"
6		"strings"
7		"time"
8	
9		"w2t86/internal/models"
10	)
11	
12	// UserRepository provides database operations for the users table.
13	type UserRepository struct {
14		db *sql.DB
15	}
16	
17	// NewUserRepository returns a UserRepository backed by the given database.
18	func NewUserRepository(db *sql.DB) *UserRepository {
19		return &UserRepository{db: db}
20	}
21	
22	// userCols is the canonical ordered column list returned by every user SELECT.
23	// All scan helpers must match this exact order.
24	const userCols = `id, username, email, password_hash, role,
25		       failed_attempts, locked_until, date_of_birth,
26		       full_name, full_name_idx,
27		       external_id, external_id_idx,
28		       created_at, updated_at, deleted_at,
29		       must_change_password`
30	
31	// Create inserts a new user and returns the populated model.
32	func (r *UserRepository) Create(username, email, passwordHash, role string) (*models.User, error) {
33		q := `INSERT INTO users (username, email, password_hash, role)
34			VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
35			RETURNING ` + userCols
36	
37		row := r.db.QueryRow(q, username, email, passwordHash, role)
38		return scanUser(row)
39	}
40	
41	// GetByID returns the user with the given id, or an error if not found.
42	func (r *UserRepository) GetByID(id int64) (*models.User, error) {
43		q := `SELECT ` + userCols + ` FROM users WHERE id = ? AND deleted_at IS NULL`
44		row := r.db.QueryRow(q, id)
45		return scanUser(row)
46	}
47	
48	// GetByUsername returns the user with the given username, or an error if not found.
49	func (r *UserRepository) GetByUsername(username string) (*models.User, error) {
50		q := `SELECT ` + userCols + ` FROM users WHERE username = ? AND deleted_at IS NULL`
51		row := r.db.QueryRow(q, username)
52		return scanUser(row)
53	}
54	
55	// IncrementFailedAttempts adds one to failed_attempts for the given user.
56	func (r *UserRepository) IncrementFailedAttempts(id int64) error {
57		const q = `UPDATE users SET failed_attempts = failed_attempts + 1, updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?`
58		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, id)
59		return err
60	}
61	
62	// LockUntil sets locked_until to the given time for the given user.
63	func (r *UserRepository) LockUntil(id int64, until time.Time) error {
64		const q = `UPDATE users SET locked_until = ?, updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?`
65		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, until.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339), id)
66		return err
67	}
68	
69	// ResetFailedAttempts zeroes failed_attempts and clears locked_until.
70	func (r *UserRepository) ResetFailedAttempts(id int64) error {
71		const q = `UPDATE users SET failed_attempts = 0, locked_until = NULL, updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?`
72		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, id)
73		return err
74	}
75	
76	// List returns up to limit users starting at offset, excluding soft-deleted rows.
77	func (r *UserRepository) List(limit, offset int) ([]models.User, error) {
78		q := `SELECT ` + userCols + `
79			FROM   users
80			WHERE  deleted_at IS NULL
81			ORDER  BY id
82			LIMIT  ? OFFSET ?`
83	
84		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, limit, offset)
85		if err != nil {
86			return nil, err
87		}
88		defer rows.Close()
89	
90		var users []models.User
91		for rows.Next() {
92			u, err := scanUserRow(rows)
93			if err != nil {
94				return nil, err
95			}
96			users = append(users, *u)
97		}
98		return users, rows.Err()
99	}
100	
101	// Update applies the given field map to the user identified by id.
102	// Only columns present in the map are changed; updated_at is always refreshed.
103	// Allowed keys: username, email, password_hash, role.
104	func (r *UserRepository) Update(id int64, fields map[string]interface{}) error {
105		if len(fields) == 0 {
106			return nil
107		}
108	
109		allowed := map[string]bool{
110			"username":      true,
111			"email":         true,
112			"password_hash": true,
113			"role":          true,
114		}
115	
116		setClauses := make([]string, 0, len(fields)+1)
117		args := make([]interface{}, 0, len(fields)+2)
118	
119		for col, val := range fields {
120			if !allowed[col] {
121				return fmt.Errorf("repository: Update: unknown or disallowed column %q", col)
122			}
123			setClauses = append(setClauses, col+" = ?")
124			args = append(args, val)
125		}
126		setClauses = append(setClauses, "updated_at = datetime('now')")
127		args = append(args, id)
128	
129		q := "UPDATE users SET " + strings.Join(setClauses, ", ") + " WHERE id = ? AND deleted_at IS NULL"
130		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, args...)
131		return err
132	}
133	
134	// SetFullName stores the encrypted full_name and its HMAC blind index.
135	// fullName is the encrypted ciphertext (enc: prefix + base64 AES-GCM blob).
136	// fullNameIdx is the HMAC-SHA256 blind index used for SQL equality matching.
137	func (r *UserRepository) SetFullName(userID int64, fullName, fullNameIdx string) error {
138		const q = `UPDATE users SET full_name = ?, full_name_idx = ?, updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ? AND deleted_at IS NULL`
139		var idxVal interface{}
140		if fullNameIdx != "" {
141			idxVal = fullNameIdx
142		}
143		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, fullName, idxVal, userID)
144		return err
145	}
146	
147	// SetExternalID stores the encrypted external_id and its HMAC blind index.
148	// externalID is the encrypted ciphertext; externalIDIdx is the HMAC blind index.
149	func (r *UserRepository) SetExternalID(userID int64, externalID, externalIDIdx string) error {
150		const q = `UPDATE users SET external_id = ?, external_id_idx = ?, updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ? AND deleted_at IS NULL`
151		var idxVal interface{}
152		if externalIDIdx != "" {
153			idxVal = externalIDIdx
154		}
155		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, externalID, idxVal, userID)
156		return err
157	}
158	
159	// ClearMustChangePassword sets must_change_password = 0 for the given user
160	// after they have successfully rotated their password.
161	func (r *UserRepository) ClearMustChangePassword(userID int64) error {
162		const q = `UPDATE users SET must_change_password = 0, updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?`
163		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, userID)
164		return err
165	}
166	
167	// SoftDelete sets deleted_at on the user row.
168	func (r *UserRepository) SoftDelete(id int64) error {
169		const q = `UPDATE users SET deleted_at = datetime('now'), updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ? AND deleted_at IS NULL`
170		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, id)
171		return err
172	}
173	
174	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
175	// helpers
176	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
177	
178	type scanner interface {
179		Scan(dest ...interface{}) error
180	}
181	
182	// scanUser scans a single-row result into a User.
183	// Column order must match userCols exactly.
184	func scanUser(s scanner) (*models.User, error) {
185		u := &models.User{}
186		err := s.Scan(
187			&u.ID, &u.Username, &u.Email, &u.PasswordHash, &u.Role,
188			&u.FailedAttempts, &u.LockedUntil, &u.DateOfBirth,
189			&u.FullName, &u.FullNameIdx,
190			&u.ExternalID, &u.ExternalIDIdx,
191			&u.CreatedAt, &u.UpdatedAt, &u.DeletedAt,
192			&u.MustChangePassword,
193		)
194		if err != nil {
195			return nil, err
196		}
197		return u, nil
198	}
199	
200	// scanUserRow scans the current row from a *sql.Rows result set into a User.
201	// Column order must match userCols exactly.
202	func scanUserRow(rows *sql.Rows) (*models.User, error) {
203		u := &models.User{}
204		err := rows.Scan(
205			&u.ID, &u.Username, &u.Email, &u.PasswordHash, &u.Role,
206			&u.FailedAttempts, &u.LockedUntil, &u.DateOfBirth,
207			&u.FullName, &u.FullNameIdx,
208			&u.ExternalID, &u.ExternalIDIdx,
209			&u.CreatedAt, &u.UpdatedAt, &u.DeletedAt,
210			&u.MustChangePassword,
211		)
212		if err != nil {
213			return nil, err
214		}
215		return u, nil
216	}
217

1	package repository
2	
3	import (
4		"crypto/rand"
5		"database/sql"
6		"encoding/hex"
7		"errors"
8		"fmt"
9		"time"
10	
11		"w2t86/internal/models"
12	)
13	
14	// ErrShareExpired is returned when a share token exists but has passed its expiry time.
15	var ErrShareExpired = errors.New("share link has expired")
16	
17	// ErrAlreadyRated is returned when a user attempts to rate a material they
18	// have already rated.  Per-prompt rule: each textbook can be rated once per
19	// student.
20	var ErrAlreadyRated = errors.New("you have already rated this material")
21	
22	// EngagementRepository provides database operations for browse history, ratings,
23	// comments, comment reports, and favorites lists/items.
24	type EngagementRepository struct {
25		db *sql.DB
26	}
27	
28	// NewEngagementRepository returns an EngagementRepository backed by the given database.
29	func NewEngagementRepository(db *sql.DB) *EngagementRepository {
30		return &EngagementRepository{db: db}
31	}
32	
33	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
34	// Browse history
35	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
36	
37	// RecordVisit inserts (or replaces) a browse history row for the given user + material.
38	func (r *EngagementRepository) RecordVisit(userID, materialID int64) error {
39		const q = `
40			INSERT INTO browse_history (user_id, material_id, visited_at)
41			VALUES (?, ?, datetime('now'))`
42		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, userID, materialID)
43		return err
44	}
45	
46	// GetHistory returns the most-recently visited materials for a user.
47	func (r *EngagementRepository) GetHistory(userID int64, limit int) ([]models.BrowseHistory, error) {
48		const q = `
49			SELECT id, user_id, material_id, visited_at
50			FROM   browse_history
51			WHERE  user_id = ?
52			ORDER  BY visited_at DESC
53			LIMIT  ?`
54	
55		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, userID, limit)
56		if err != nil {
57			return nil, err
58		}
59		defer rows.Close()
60	
61		var items []models.BrowseHistory
62		for rows.Next() {
63			var bh models.BrowseHistory
64			if err := rows.Scan(&bh.ID, &bh.UserID, &bh.MaterialID, &bh.VisitedAt); err != nil {
65				return nil, err
66			}
67			items = append(items, bh)
68		}
69		return items, rows.Err()
70	}
71	
72	// GetHistoryItems returns browse history joined with material titles for a user.
73	func (r *EngagementRepository) GetHistoryItems(userID int64, limit int) ([]models.HistoryItem, error) {
74		const q = `
75			SELECT bh.material_id, m.title, bh.visited_at
76			FROM   browse_history bh
77			JOIN   materials m ON m.id = bh.material_id
78			WHERE  bh.user_id = ?
79			ORDER  BY bh.visited_at DESC
80			LIMIT  ?`
81	
82		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, userID, limit)
83		if err != nil {
84			return nil, err
85		}
86		defer rows.Close()
87	
88		var items []models.HistoryItem
89		for rows.Next() {
90			var hi models.HistoryItem
91			if err := rows.Scan(&hi.MaterialID, &hi.MaterialTitle, &hi.VisitedAt); err != nil {
92				return nil, err
93			}
94			items = append(items, hi)
95		}
96		return items, rows.Err()
97	}
98	
99	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
100	// Ratings
101	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
102	
103	// InsertRating records a star rating for a material/user pair exactly once.
104	// Returns ErrAlreadyRated if the user has already rated this material.
105	func (r *EngagementRepository) InsertRating(materialID, userID int64, stars int) error {
106		const q = `
107			INSERT OR IGNORE INTO ratings (material_id, user_id, stars, created_at)
108			VALUES (?, ?, ?, datetime('now'))`
109		res, err := r.db.Exec(q, materialID, userID, stars)
110		if err != nil {
111			return err
112		}
113		n, _ := res.RowsAffected()
114		if n == 0 {
115			return ErrAlreadyRated
116		}
117		return nil
118	}
119	
120	// GetRating returns the rating a specific user gave to a material, or nil if none.
121	func (r *EngagementRepository) GetRating(materialID, userID int64) (*models.Rating, error) {
122		const q = `
123			SELECT id, material_id, user_id, stars, created_at
124			FROM   ratings
125			WHERE  material_id = ? AND user_id = ?`
126	
127		row := r.db.QueryRow(q, materialID, userID)
128		rt := &models.Rating{}
129		err := row.Scan(&rt.ID, &rt.MaterialID, &rt.UserID, &rt.Stars, &rt.CreatedAt)
130		if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
131			return nil, nil
132		}
133		if err != nil {
134			return nil, err
135		}
136		return rt, nil
137	}
138	
139	// GetAverageRating returns the average star rating and total count for a material.
140	func (r *EngagementRepository) GetAverageRating(materialID int64) (float64, int, error) {
141		const q = `
142			SELECT COALESCE(AVG(CAST(stars AS REAL)), 0.0), COUNT(*)
143			FROM   ratings
144			WHERE  material_id = ?`
145	
146		var avg float64
147		var count int
148		err := r.db.QueryRow(q, materialID).Scan(&avg, &count)
149		return avg, count, err
150	}
151	
152	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
153	// Comments
154	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
155	
156	// CreateComment inserts a new comment and returns the populated model.
157	func (r *EngagementRepository) CreateComment(materialID, userID int64, body string, linkCount int) (*models.Comment, error) {
158		const q = `
159			INSERT INTO comments (material_id, user_id, body, link_count, status, report_count)
160			VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, 'visible', 0)
161			RETURNING id, material_id, user_id, body, link_count, status, report_count, created_at, updated_at`
162	
163		row := r.db.QueryRow(q, materialID, userID, body, linkCount)
164		c := &models.Comment{}
165		err := row.Scan(&c.ID, &c.MaterialID, &c.UserID, &c.Body, &c.LinkCount,
166			&c.Status, &c.ReportCount, &c.CreatedAt, &c.UpdatedAt)
167		if err != nil {
168			return nil, err
169		}
170		return c, nil
171	}
172	
173	// GetComments returns comments for a material, optionally including collapsed ones.
174	func (r *EngagementRepository) GetComments(materialID int64, includeCollapsed bool, limit, offset int) ([]models.Comment, error) {
175		var q string
176		if includeCollapsed {
177			q = `
178				SELECT id, material_id, user_id, body, link_count, status, report_count, created_at, updated_at
179				FROM   comments
180				WHERE  material_id = ?
181				ORDER  BY created_at DESC
182				LIMIT  ? OFFSET ?`
183		} else {
184			q = `
185				SELECT id, material_id, user_id, body, link_count, status, report_count, created_at, updated_at
186				FROM   comments
187				WHERE  material_id = ? AND status != 'removed'
188				ORDER  BY created_at DESC
189				LIMIT  ? OFFSET ?`
190		}
191	
192		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, materialID, limit, offset)
193		if err != nil {
194			return nil, err
195		}
196		defer rows.Close()
197	
198		var comments []models.Comment
199		for rows.Next() {
200			var c models.Comment
201			if err := rows.Scan(&c.ID, &c.MaterialID, &c.UserID, &c.Body, &c.LinkCount,
202				&c.Status, &c.ReportCount, &c.CreatedAt, &c.UpdatedAt); err != nil {
203				return nil, err
204			}
205			comments = append(comments, c)
206		}
207		return comments, rows.Err()
208	}
209	
210	// ReportComment records a report for a comment.
211	// After inserting the report, it counts unique reporters; if >= 3, the comment
212	// status is set to 'collapsed'.
213	func (r *EngagementRepository) ReportComment(commentID, reportedBy int64, reason string) error {
214		tx, err := r.db.Begin()
215		if err != nil {
216			return err
217		}
218		defer func() {
219			if err != nil {
220				_ = tx.Rollback()
221			}
222		}()
223	
224		const insertReport = `
225			INSERT OR IGNORE INTO comment_reports (comment_id, reported_by, reason, created_at)
226			VALUES (?, ?, ?, datetime('now'))`
227		_, err = tx.Exec(insertReport, commentID, reportedBy, reason)
228		if err != nil {
229			return err
230		}
231	
232		var uniqueReporters int
233		const countQ = `SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT reported_by) FROM comment_reports WHERE comment_id = ?`
234		err = tx.QueryRow(countQ, commentID).Scan(&uniqueReporters)
235		if err != nil {
236			return err
237		}
238	
239		if uniqueReporters >= 3 {
240			const collapseQ = `UPDATE comments SET status = 'collapsed', updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ? AND status = 'visible'`
241			if _, err = tx.Exec(collapseQ, commentID); err != nil {
242				return err
243			}
244		}
245	
246		return tx.Commit()
247	}
248	
249	// CountRecentComments returns the number of comments a user has posted since the given time.
250	func (r *EngagementRepository) CountRecentComments(userID int64, since time.Time) (int, error) {
251		const q = `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM comments WHERE user_id = ? AND created_at >= ?`
252		var count int
253		err := r.db.QueryRow(q, userID, since.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)).Scan(&count)
254		return count, err
255	}
256	
257	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
258	// Favorites
259	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
260	
261	// CreateList creates a new favorites list for a user.
262	func (r *EngagementRepository) CreateList(userID int64, name, visibility string) (*models.FavoritesList, error) {
263		const q = `
264			INSERT INTO favorites_lists (user_id, name, visibility)
265			VALUES (?, ?, ?)
266			RETURNING id, user_id, name, visibility, share_token, share_expires_at, created_at`
267	
268		row := r.db.QueryRow(q, userID, name, visibility)
269		fl := &models.FavoritesList{}
270		err := row.Scan(&fl.ID, &fl.UserID, &fl.Name, &fl.Visibility,
271			&fl.ShareToken, &fl.ShareExpiresAt, &fl.CreatedAt)
272		if err != nil {
273			return nil, err
274		}
275		return fl, nil
276	}
277	
278	// GetLists returns all favorites lists for a user.
279	func (r *EngagementRepository) GetLists(userID int64) ([]models.FavoritesList, error) {
280		const q = `
281			SELECT id, user_id, name, visibility, share_token, share_expires_at, created_at
282			FROM   favorites_lists
283			WHERE  user_id = ?
284			ORDER  BY created_at DESC`
285	
286		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, userID)
287		if err != nil {
288			return nil, err
289		}
290		defer rows.Close()
291	
292		var lists []models.FavoritesList
293		for rows.Next() {
294			var fl models.FavoritesList
295			if err := rows.Scan(&fl.ID, &fl.UserID, &fl.Name, &fl.Visibility,
296				&fl.ShareToken, &fl.ShareExpiresAt, &fl.CreatedAt); err != nil {
297				return nil, err
298			}
299			lists = append(lists, fl)
300		}
301		return lists, rows.Err()
302	}
303	
304	// GetListByID returns a single favorites list by its primary key.
305	func (r *EngagementRepository) GetListByID(id int64) (*models.FavoritesList, error) {
306		const q = `
307			SELECT id, user_id, name, visibility, share_token, share_expires_at, created_at
308			FROM   favorites_lists
309			WHERE  id = ?`
310	
311		row := r.db.QueryRow(q, id)
312		fl := &models.FavoritesList{}
313		err := row.Scan(&fl.ID, &fl.UserID, &fl.Name, &fl.Visibility,
314			&fl.ShareToken, &fl.ShareExpiresAt, &fl.CreatedAt)
315		if err != nil {
316			return nil, err
317		}
318		return fl, nil
319	}
320	
321	// GetListByShareToken returns the favorites list matching the given share token.
322	// Returns ErrShareExpired if the token exists but has expired, sql.ErrNoRows if
323	// no such token exists at all.
324	func (r *EngagementRepository) GetListByShareToken(token string) (*models.FavoritesList, error) {
325		const q = `
326			SELECT id, user_id, name, visibility, share_token, share_expires_at, created_at
327			FROM   favorites_lists
328			WHERE  share_token = ?`
329	
330		row := r.db.QueryRow(q, token)
331		fl := &models.FavoritesList{}
332		err := row.Scan(&fl.ID, &fl.UserID, &fl.Name, &fl.Visibility,
333			&fl.ShareToken, &fl.ShareExpiresAt, &fl.CreatedAt)
334		if err != nil {
335			return nil, err
336		}
337		// Check expiry in Go so callers can distinguish expired vs not-found.
338		if fl.ShareExpiresAt != nil {
339			if exp, parseErr := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, *fl.ShareExpiresAt); parseErr == nil {
340				if time.Now().After(exp) {
341					return nil, ErrShareExpired
342				}
343			}
344		}
345		return fl, nil
346	}
347	
348	// AddToList inserts a material into a favorites list (ignores duplicates).
349	func (r *EngagementRepository) AddToList(listID, materialID int64) error {
350		const q = `
351			INSERT OR IGNORE INTO favorites_items (list_id, material_id, added_at)
352			VALUES (?, ?, datetime('now'))`
353		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, listID, materialID)
354		return err
355	}
356	
357	// RemoveFromList deletes a material from a favorites list.
358	func (r *EngagementRepository) RemoveFromList(listID, materialID int64) error {
359		const q = `DELETE FROM favorites_items WHERE list_id = ? AND material_id = ?`
360		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, listID, materialID)
361		return err
362	}
363	
364	// GetListItems returns all items in a favorites list.
365	func (r *EngagementRepository) GetListItems(listID int64) ([]models.FavoritesItem, error) {
366		const q = `
367			SELECT id, list_id, material_id, added_at
368			FROM   favorites_items
369			WHERE  list_id = ?
370			ORDER  BY added_at DESC`
371	
372		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, listID)
373		if err != nil {
374			return nil, err
375		}
376		defer rows.Close()
377	
378		var items []models.FavoritesItem
379		for rows.Next() {
380			var fi models.FavoritesItem
381			if err := rows.Scan(&fi.ID, &fi.ListID, &fi.MaterialID, &fi.AddedAt); err != nil {
382				return nil, err
383			}
384			items = append(items, fi)
385		}
386		return items, rows.Err()
387	}
388	
389	// GenerateShareToken creates a crypto-random share token, stores it on the
390	// favorites list, and returns the raw token string.
391	func (r *EngagementRepository) GenerateShareToken(listID int64, expiresAt time.Time) (string, error) {
392		b := make([]byte, 24)
393		if _, err := rand.Read(b); err != nil {
394			return "", fmt.Errorf("repository: GenerateShareToken: rand: %w", err)
395		}
396		token := hex.EncodeToString(b)
397	
398		const q = `
399			UPDATE favorites_lists
400			SET    share_token = ?, share_expires_at = ?
401			WHERE  id = ?`
402		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, token, expiresAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339), listID)
403		if err != nil {
404			return "", err
405		}
406		return token, nil
407	}
408	
409	// UpdateListVisibility sets the visibility field on a favorites list.
410	func (r *EngagementRepository) UpdateListVisibility(listID int64, visibility string) error {
411		const q = `UPDATE favorites_lists SET visibility = ? WHERE id = ?`
412		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, visibility, listID)
413		return err
414	}
415

1	package config
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/hex"
5		"errors"
6		"fmt"
7		"os"
8	)
9	
10	// Config holds all runtime configuration for the portal.
11	type Config struct {
12		Port          string // HTTP listen port, default "3000"
13		DBPath        string // SQLite file path, default "data/portal.db"
14		EncryptionKey string // 32-byte hex key for AES-256-GCM field encryption
15		SessionSecret string // secret used to sign/verify session tokens
16		AppEnv        string // "development" | "production"
17		BannedWords   string // comma-separated list of prohibited comment words
18	}
19	
20	// Load reads configuration from environment variables, applying defaults where
21	// a variable is absent or empty.
22	func Load() *Config {
23		return &Config{
24			Port:          envOrDefault("PORT", "3000"),
25			DBPath:        envOrDefault("DB_PATH", "data/portal.db"),
26			EncryptionKey: envOrDefault("ENCRYPTION_KEY", ""),
27			SessionSecret: envOrDefault("SESSION_SECRET", ""),
28			AppEnv:        envOrDefault("APP_ENV", "development"),
29			BannedWords:   envOrDefault("BANNED_WORDS", ""),
30		}
31	}
32	
33	// Validate returns an error if any required secret configuration is absent or
34	// malformed.  Call this at startup so the application fails fast rather than
35	// running with an invalid encryption key or session secret.
36	//
37	// ENCRYPTION_KEY must be a 64-character lower-case hex string (32 raw bytes).
38	// Any other value — including empty — is rejected; there is no plaintext
39	// fallback once the system has started.
40	func (c *Config) Validate() error {
41		var errs []error
42		if c.EncryptionKey == "" {
43			errs = append(errs, errors.New("ENCRYPTION_KEY must be set (64 hex chars / 32 bytes)"))
44		} else {
45			raw, err := hex.DecodeString(c.EncryptionKey)
46			if err != nil {
47				errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("ENCRYPTION_KEY is not valid hex: %w", err))
48			} else if len(raw) != 32 {
49				errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("ENCRYPTION_KEY must decode to 32 bytes (got %d); supply a 64-char hex string", len(raw)))
50			}
51		}
52		if c.SessionSecret == "" {
53			errs = append(errs, errors.New("SESSION_SECRET must be set"))
54		}
55		return errors.Join(errs...)
56	}
57	
58	// envOrDefault returns the value of the named environment variable, or
59	// fallback when the variable is unset or empty.
60	func envOrDefault(key, fallback string) string {
61		if v := os.Getenv(key); v != "" {
62			return v
63		}
64		return fallback
65	}
66
